name: Casserole of Salt Cod and Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------GARNISH----------------------------------
1 lg Egg, hard-cooked, shelled
and cut into thin wedges
12 md Oil-cured black olives (unpitted)
1. Soak the cod in the refrigerator for 24 hours, changing the water several times.
2. Drain and rinse the cod well.
3. Place the cod in a large heavy saucepan, add the boiling water, set over moderate heat, cover, and simmer for 10-to-12 minutes until it flakes easily with a fork.
4. Remove from the saucepan and rinse well; flake the cod, removing any skin or bone pieces.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
6. In a large heavy skillet, warm the butter and 1 tablespoon of oil over moderate heat for 1 minute.
7. Add the onion (sliced into rings) and stir-fry for 8-to-10 minutes until limp and golden; do not brown.
8. Remove the onion from the skillet and set aside.
9. Add the remaining oil to the skillet, add the potatoes, and stir-fry for about 5 minutes until golden.
10. Layer half of the potatoes in a well-buttered 2-quart casserole or au gratin pan and sprinkle with a little minced parsley and pepper.
11. Add one-third of the onion, one-half of the cod, and another scattering of parsley and pepper.
12. Repeat the layering, finishing with onion rings on top.
13. Sprinkle with the remaining pepper and all but 1 tablespoon of the parsley.
14. Bake the casserole uncovered for 35-to-40 minutes or until lightly browned.
15. Garnish with wedges of hard-cooked egg and oil-cured black olives, placed artfully on top of the casserole, plus a final light scattering of minced parsley.
